text.fullText: We support broker transparency. Carriers and owner operators are treated very unfairly and egregiously taken advantage of by brokers when it comes to not only providing decent rates, but also when held to cover agreed accessorial charges such as detention and layover. The rates provided do not even cover carriers’ expenses these days, while brokers are pocketing the majority of rates provided by customers. How is this legal? In what way is it appropriate for brokers to hoard the majority of rates paid by customers when they are not only third party to the actual services of freight movement provided, but also do not have the liability or expenses that come with the freight service provided? Most of the liability is on the carrier. On top of it all, when applicable to pay, carriers have to fight and even take litigation measures to receive detention and layover pay that are contractually agreed upon with the brokers. This madness needs serious correction, and it begins with broker transparency.
